sinceinstall.description: Difference between the current meter reading and the meter reading when this AssetMeter's asset was moved into its current configuration.
sinceinstall.title: Since Install

startassetfeatureid.description: A previously defined feature or relationship used, in conjunction with the start offset, to determine the start measure for this meter.
startassetfeatureid.title: Start Asset Feature ID

startmeasure.description: Absolute distance taken from the start of the linear asset to where this meter is defined.  This can be manually entered or calculated by entering a start reference point and start offset.
startmeasure.title: Start Measure

ahiassetmeterstatusicon.description: Status icon calculated from last reading and measure point
ahiassetmeterstatusicon.title: Status icon

domainid.description: This domain information is used to validate a new CHARACTERISTIC measurement.
domainid.title: Domain

endoffsetunitid.description: Unit of measure for the offset (before or after) from the end reference point of this meter.
endoffsetunitid.title: Unit of End Offset

sincelastinspect.description: Difference between the current meter reading and the meter reading when the last work order of type INSPECTION was opened or closed as determined by application setup.
sincelastinspect.title: Since Last Inspection

endzoffset.description: Distance above or below this linear asset.  For example, if a sign is 10 feet above the road, the  Z-Offset is 10 feet.  This value is measured from the Z  Reference Point and can be positive (above) or negative (below).
endzoffset.title: End Z Offset

endbasemeasure.description: An absolute measure that is calculated by converting from the end measure using a defined conversion method.  Conversion methods are defined in the Assets application using the Add/Modify Conversions action.
endbasemeasure.title: End Base Measure

active.description: Is this asset's meter available to accept manual or rolled down readings, or to be used in PM or CM work order generation?
active.title: Active

changedate.description: Date and time record was last modified.
changedate.title: Changed Date

previousreadingdate.description: non-persistent attribute that is the date of the reading just previous to the meter reading being added or updated.
previousreadingdate.title: Previous Reading Date

sincelastrepair.description: Difference between current meter reading and the meter reading when the last work order was opened or closed as determined by the application setup.
sincelastrepair.title: Since Last Repair

plusgemissionimpact.description: The impact of the emission such as fugitive or direct emission
plusgemissionimpact.title: Emission Impact

startoffsetunitid.description: Unit of measure for the offset (before or after) from the start reference point of this meter.
startoffsetunitid.title: Unit of Start Offset

startfeatureid.description: unique identifier for the feature that marks the start of the section of the linear asset for which this meter applies
startfeatureid.title: StartFeature ID

startfeature.description: The feature (e.g. mile marker) that is used to identify where, on the linear asset, the meter reading was taken.
startfeature.title: Start Feature

measureunitid.description: Measure unit for the meter when attached to this asset, for example LITERS or RPM
measureunitid.title: Unit of Measure

metername.description: Identifier for the meter attached to the asset, for example HOURS or KILOMETERS.
metername.title: Meter

startoffset.description: Distance used in conjunction with the start reference point to determine where the meter resides.
startoffset.title: Start Offset

endassetfeatureid.description: A previously defined feature or relationship used, in conjunction with the end offset, to determine the end measure for this meter.
endassetfeatureid.title: End Asset Feature ID

plusgconsenttype.description: The type of environmental consent, such as emissions to water, emissions to air, or energy usage.
plusgconsenttype.title: Consent Type

dorollover.description: non-persistent attribute to indicate if a new reading should cause the meter to roll over
dorollover.title: Rollover

cotwomeasureunitid.description: The unit of measure such as kilograms or gallons
cotwomeasureunitid.title: CO2 Measurement Unit

slidingwindowsize.description: The number of readings, days, weeks or months to include in a sliding average meter unit calculation.
slidingwindowsize.title: Sliding Window Size

pointnum.description: identifier of this AssetMeter's related condition monitoring point.
pointnum.title: Point

ahiassetmeterstatus.description: Status calculated from last reading and measure point
ahiassetmeterstatus.title: Status

average.description: Average units per day for this meter
average.title: Average Units/Day

endfeature.description: The feature (e.g. mile marker) that is used to identify where, on the linear asset, the meter reading was taken.
endfeature.title: End Feature

lastreadingdate.description: Date of the last reading for this meter.
lastreadingdate.title: Last Reading Date

linearassetmeterid.description: Part of the primary key to differentiate Linear asset meters only.
linearassetmeterid.title: Linear Specification Id

endoffset.description: Distance used in conjunction with the end reference point to determine where the meter resides.
endoffset.title: End Offset

lastreading.description: Last reading taken for this meter. This should be the same as the most recent meter reading. An asset that was used before it was added to the system will have an asset meter reading. The last meter reading for an existing asset can be used as the initial meter reading for that asset in the system.
lastreading.title: Last Reading

assetnum.description: Asset number to which the meter is attached
assetnum.title: Asset

endyoffsetref.description: The point from which the Y Offset is measured (e.g. centerline, edge, etc).   The user can create a domain for this purpose.  The value entered will not be used in calculations, it is simply used to locate the feature in the field.
endyoffsetref.title: End Y Offset Referent

startzoffset.description: Distance above or below this linear asset.  For example, if a sign is 10 feet above the road, the  Z-Offset is 10 feet.  This value is measured from the Z  Reference Point and can be positive (above) or negative (below).
startzoffset.title: Start Z Offset

rollover.description: Point at which the asset's meter returns to the its mininum value. Rollover applies to CONTINUOUS meters only.
rollover.title: Rollover

startzoffsetref.description: Reference point from which the start Z-Offset (distance above or below the linear asset) is measured.
startzoffsetref.title: Start Z Offset Referent

plusgemissiontype.description: The type of emission such as emission to air, emission to ground
plusgemissiontype.title: Emission Type

plusgenvconmon.description: Indicates whether monitoring for environmental consents, such as monitoring the quality of emissions to air, is associated with the meter for this asset.
plusgenvconmon.title: Environmental Consent Monitoring

startyoffsetref.description: The point from which the Y Offset is measured (e.g. centerline, edge, etc).   The user can create a domain for this purpose.  The value entered will not be used in calculations, it is simply used to locate the feature in the field.
startyoffsetref.title: Start Y Offset Referent

avgcalcmethod.description: Method used to calculate the average meter units per day: STATIC, ALL or SLIDING.
avgcalcmethod.title: Average Calculation Method

changeby.description: Name of user who last modified this record.
changeby.title: Changed By

endmeasure.description: Absolute distance taken from the start of the linear asset to where this meter is defined.  This can be manually entered or calculated by entering an end reference point and end offset.
endmeasure.title: End Measure

startyoffset.description: Perpendicular distance from this linear asset.  For example, if a sign is 10 feet to the right of the road, the Y-Offset is 10 feet.  This value is measured from the Y Reference Point and can be positive (right) or negative (left).
startyoffset.title: Start Y Offset

lastreadinginspctr.description: inspector for this meter's most recent meter reading
lastreadinginspctr.title: Last Reading Inspector

endfeatureid.description: unique identifier for the feature that marks the end of the section of the linear asset for which this meter applies
endfeatureid.title: EndFeature ID

endyoffset.description: Perpendicular distance from this linear asset.  For example, if a sign is 10 feet to the right of the road, the Y-Offset is 10 feet.  This value is measured from the Y Reference Point and can be positive (right) or negative (left).
endyoffset.title: End Y Offset

lifetodate.description: This value tracks the total use of an asset over its life span. It will always be equal to or exceed the last reading value and is used to determine the meter-based PM frequencies. If a meter does not roll over and is never replaced or reset, this value is the same as the last reading.  Even if the meter rolls over or the physical meter is replaced or reset, this value continues to increase because the value is based on the asset.
lifetodate.title: Life to Date for Asset

plusgemissioncat.description: The category of emission such as leak, spill or vent
plusgemissioncat.title: Emission Category

ahiassetmeterstatuscolor.description: Status color calculated from last reading and measure point
ahiassetmeterstatuscolor.title: Status color

sincelastoverhaul.description: Difference between the current meter reading and the meter reading when the last work order of type OVERHAUL was opened or closed as determined by application setup.
sincelastoverhaul.title: Since Last Overhaul

previousreading.description: non-persistent attribute that is the reading just previous to the meter reading being added or updated.
previousreading.title: Previous Reading

readingtype.description: reading type (CONTINUOUS or DELTA) for this meter.
readingtype.title: Reading Type

rolldownsource.description: Identifies whether or not an asset's meter accepts a reading delta from a parent asset's or location's meter. A value of NONE indicates that this Meter does not accept rolled down parent readings.
rolldownsource.title: Accept Rolldown From

plusghoseinspectby.description: The person who inspected the hose.
plusghoseinspectby.title: Inspected By

pluscsumeu.description: This field is summed with the %Span, %URV, and %Reading EU fields to obtain the total accuracy of the asset.
pluscsumeu.title: Accuracy EU

pluscsumspan.description: This field is summed with the %Reading, %URV, and Accuracy EU fields to obtain the total accuracy of the asset.
pluscsumspan.title: %SPAN

startmeasure.description: A value that identifies the start of the linear asset.  The absolute value of the end measure minus the start measure will determine the length of the linear asset.  The start measure and the end measure also determine the boundary measures for any features or relationships applied to the asset.
startmeasure.title: Start Measure

pluscisinhousecal.description: When selected indicates that this tool is calibrated in house. The default is cleared.
pluscisinhousecal.title: Internal Calibration

pluscvendor.description: Vendor/Facility that does the actual calibration.
pluscvendor.title: Calibration Vendor

plusgliftequreference.description: The reference to documentation  or to an identifier that is associated with the type of lifting equipment that is used.
plusgliftequreference.title: Reference

plusgaccequreference.description: The reference to documentation or to an identifier that is associated with the type of access equipment that is used.
plusgaccequreference.title: Reference

adjustedlifedate.description: The date that the asset reaches the adjusted useful life date. This date is calculated by plans or asset expected life.
adjustedlifedate.title: Adjusted life

plusgfmperson.description: The person who is leading the failure analysis effort.
plusgfmperson.title: Failure Analysis Leader

pluscoprgeto.description: Enter the maximum numeric value of the operating range of the tool. This field is used in conjuction with the Operating Range From field.
pluscoprgeto.title: To

methodname.description: The scoring method applied to the asset.
methodname.title: Scoring Method

plusgtargetavail.description: A percentage that represents the required availability of the asset in order to meet operational commitments. For example, if the asset is available 24 hours a day, and you need to use it 23 of those 24 hours, the target availability is 95.833. This value can have 3 decimal positions. You can change this value while the asset record is in any status.
plusgtargetavail.title: Target Availability

plusgfmcomplete.description: The date when the failure analysis is set to be completed.
plusgfmcomplete.title: Complete

plusghosereference.description: The reference to documentation or to an identifier that is associated with the type of hose that is used.
plusghosereference.title: Reference

plusgliftequtype.description: The type of lifting equipment that is used.
plusgliftequtype.title: Lift Equipment Type

depreciationpending.description: Indicates that generation of the depreciation schedule is pending final receipt or invoice of the PO that this rotating asset was purchased on.
depreciationpending.title: Depreciation Pending

groupname.description: All meters belonging to this asset's metergroup are automatically associated with this Asset in the AssetMeter object. Additions to the meter group may also be added to this Asset's AssetMeters.
groupname.title: Meter Group

plusgbenchsource.description: The source of the benchmark mean time between failures for the asset. Valid values are in the PLUSGBENCHSOURCE domain.
plusgbenchsource.title: Benchmark Source

pluscismteclass.description: If the Is (M&TE) checkbox is selected, specify the classification for tool.
pluscismteclass.title: M&TE Classification

plusgaccequinspectby.description: The person who inspected the access equipment.
plusgaccequinspectby.title: Inspected By

plusgliftequinspectby.description: The person who inspected the lifting equipment.
plusgliftequinspectby.title: Inspected By

plusgactionsumm.description: A summary of the review actions that are associated with the asset.
plusgactionsumm.title: Review Action Summary

plusgleakdetails.description: Describes the source of the leak, and any other information about the leak.
plusgleakdetails.title: Leak Details

plussfeatureclass.description: Identifies the GIS feature class linked to the asset record.
plussfeatureclass.title: Feature Class

plusgtargetmtbf.description: The target mean time between failures for the asset, in hours. The value must be an integer.
plusgtargetmtbf.title: Target MTBF

plusciscondesc.description: This field is available when the Is Contaminated? Field is selected. It is used to enter a description of the contamination or other pertinent information.
plusciscondesc.title: Is Contaminated Description

pluscoprgefrom.description: Enter the minimum numeric value of the operating range of the tool. This field is used in conjuction with the Operating Range To field.
pluscoprgefrom.title: Operating Range From

plusghoseinspectdate.description: The date and time when the hose was last inspected.
plusghoseinspectdate.title: Last Inspection

pluscpmextdate.description: This check box is selected if the date of the associated PM is extended.
pluscpmextdate.title: Extend Date

assettype.description: The predefined type of this asset.
assettype.title: Type

conditioncode.description: The item condition of the asset
conditioncode.title: Condition Code

plusgpipprocessmedium.description: The type of medium, such as fuel or water, that the pipe is designed to carry.
plusgpipprocessmedium.title: Process Medium

plusgpiprating.description: The pressure class that the pipe is designed to withstand.
plusgpiprating.title: Rating

plusgprotectinspectby.description: The person who inspected the personal protective equipment.
plusgprotectinspectby.title: Inspected By

endmeasure.description: A value that identifies the end of the linear asset.  The absolute value of the end measure minus the start measure will determine the length of the linear asset.  The start measure and the end measure also determine the boundary measures for any features or relationships applied to the asset.
endmeasure.title: End Measure

plusgaccequinspectbyid.description: The person who inspected the access equipment.
plusgaccequinspectbyid.title: Inspected By ID

expectedlife.description: Expected life of the asset, expressed in years.  This value is based on the typical useful life that a manufacturer or industry standard recommends for an asset or asset type.
expectedlife.title: Expected life in years

plusglastchkdate.description: The date and time when the valve was last checked for leaks.
plusglastchkdate.title: Last Checked Date

plusghoseinspectbyid.description: The person who inspected the hose.
plusghoseinspectbyid.title: Inspected By ID

budgetcost.description: Budgeted Cost Of Work For The Year
budgetcost.title: Budgeted

plusgaccequinspectdate.description: The date and time when the access equipment was last inspected.
plusgaccequinspectdate.title: Last Inspection Date/Time

plussaddresscode.description: Code that identifies a service address. It must be unique by site for each service address.
plussaddresscode.title: Service Address

pluscismte.description: When selected, indicates that the selected equipment is a piece of measurement and test equipment. The default for this check box is cleared (not M&TE).
pluscismte.title: Is M&TE

assethealth.description: The health score for the asset. This score is calculated and applied to the asset from the Reliability Engineer work center.
assethealth.title: Health

pluscduedate.description: The date that the next 'Cal' work type PM will be generated
pluscduedate.title: Calibration Due Date

plusghoserating.description: The pressure that the hose is designed to withstand.
plusghoserating.title: Rating

plusgistemp.description: Indicates whether an asset is only needed for a limited time. The asset is tracked for historical purposes.

plusgfmtype.description: The type of procedural analysis that must be done to identify the potential failure of this asset. An FMEA analysis is done at the functional level. An FMECA analysis extends the FMEA analysis by including a criticality analysis, which identifies the potential failure of the asset and the consequences of the failure. Criticality-based maintenance identifies which assets are critical and what the impact would be if the assets were to fail. Criticality-based maintenance also informs procurement strategy so that inventories, and the costs associated with keeping them, are reduced but not at the expense of increased downtime.

opmodeor.name: opmodeor
opmodeor.description: if set to 1, this will cause the oslc.where clause to set the query mode to OR clause instead of AND.

oslc.properties.name: oslc.properties
oslc.properties.description: Select clause for selecting a partial view of the member resource (like an individual workorder).

_dropnulls.name: _dropnulls
_dropnulls.description: Default value is 1. If set to 0, the json response will include attributes with null values also.

transactionid.name: transactionid
transactionid.description: This is used to prevent de-duplication for create/update/delete. If this header is present then the value is compared to a list of committed transaction ids.If it has a match, then it is considered as a duplicate transaction and an HTTP conflict error is generated.

oslc.where.name: oslc.where
oslc.where.description: Where clause to filter the set of resources

x-allow-events.name: x-allow-events
x-allow-events.description: For a create/update/delete, if the request wants to suppress the object events, this request header needs to be set to 0. By default all events are fired unless programmatically stopped.

x-method-override.name: x-method-override
x-method-override.description: Due to constraints from browser the HTTP POST method is re-used for creates and updates and deletes.This request header is used to convey the specifics of the POST request. PATCH is used for update. DELETE is used for deleting the resource. BULK is used for supporting bulk operations.If this header is not present, the POST request is treated as a create request.

gbrange.name: gbrange
gbrange.description: The range clause for the group by query.

lean.name: lean
lean.description: If set to 1, this will instruct the api to work with the "lean" json format as opposed to the namespaced json that it produces by default. This option is supported only starting 7601 release of the Maximo base. Its preferred to use this format as it reduces the size of the json payload.

oslc.select.name: oslc.select
oslc.select.description:
Filter clause following the OSLC query syntax
(see below for more details on this)

gbcols.name: gbcols
gbcols.description: The comma separated group by columns.

content-type.name: content-type
content-type.description: The mime-type of the request document.

gbsortby.name: gbsortby
gbsortby.description: The order by clause for the group by query. Follows the same syntax as oslc.orderBy.

id.name: id
id.description: Unique id for object structure resource

collectioncount.name: collectioncount
collectioncount.description: If set to 1, it will instruct the api to include the total count of records that matched the query ( responseInfo.totalCount). By default it will not return that unless the maximo property mxe.oslc.collectioncount is set to 1. This is a recommended way to control collection count at the request level.

oslc.searchTerms.name: oslc.searchTerms
oslc.searchTerms.description: Record wide text search for tokens specified as this query parameter value. Example oslc.searchTerms="pump","broken"

oslc.paging.name: oslc.paging
oslc.paging.description: Instruct the server to page the query response based on the default page size. The default page size needs to be configured on the server side for this to take effect.

inlinedoc.name: inlinedoc
inlinedoc.description: Default value is 0. For attachments/doclinks, if set to 1, it will inline the attachment blob as base64 encoded string inside the json response.

apikey.name: apikey
apikey.description: Set to the users apikey. This is used when the 'api' context is used (instead of 'oslc'). Do make sure that the apikey is generated for the user. You can use the Administration app to generate an apikey

oslc.pageSize.name: oslc.pageSize
oslc.pageSize.description: Instruct the server to page the query result with the specified page size.

stablepaging.name: stablepaging
stablepaging.description: If set to true, will instruct the server to page the resource from a in session MboSet.

allornothing.name: allornothing
allornothing.description: Default value is 0. If set to 1, all the objects in the bulk call would be committed or rolled back. No partial commit.

accept.name: accept
accept.description: The mime-type of the accept document.

ignorecollectionref.name: ignorecollectionref
ignorecollectionref.description: IIf set to 1, it will drop all hrefs for child collections.

patchtype.name: patchtype
patchtype.description: This request header with the value of MERGE will instruct the server to treat a PATCH request (POST with x-method-override as PATCH) processor to merge the child objects (array of child objects) in the request with the corresponding child elements in the server. If this header is not present, the server will delete the child objects that are missing from the request.

properties.name: properties
properties.description: Select clause for selecting a partial view of the member resource.
